🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Glenstal Castle
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* kingdom of munster
*County:* County Limerick
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 19th century
*Coords:* 52.66166667, -8.38805556
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glenstal_Abbey
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Glenstal%20Castle/@52.656,-8.42,15z
*Main Image:* https://img.castlecore.uk/glenstal-castle.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* add source - https://glenstal.com/abbey/

*Description:*
Glenstal Abbey is a Catholic Benedictine monastery of the Congregation of the Annunciation located in Murroe, County Limerick, Ireland. It is dedicated to Saint Joseph and Saint Columba. In July 2024, Columba McCann was elected to serve as the seventh abbot of the community.

_Listing 1 of 19_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Ballinskelligs Priory
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* kingdom of munster
*County:* County Kerry
*Type:* abbey
*Period:* 12th century
*Coords:* 51.81527778, -10.27194444
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ballinskelligs_Abbey
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Ballinskelligs%20Priory/@51.801,-10.288,15z
*Main Image:* https://img.castlecore.uk/ballinskelligs-priory.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Ballinskelligs Abbey was an Augustinian priory,. It is located on the Iveragh Peninsula in County Kerry, Ireland. The medieval priory, built in the 12 and 13th centuries, was established as a mainland priory for the monks of Skellig Michael who were abandoning the island due to adverse weather conditions. The ruined medieval site originally consisted of church, cloister, Prior's house, refectory, and burial ground.

_Listing 2 of 19_

*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/places/ANXAkqHFfKiwnc2-xTcLeifVs9Wv3vnLjOi-mDntLV9Lr7Ba0Aw7L6jEmswDXCgBIgrUvA97sHPp6TCk1jJ_yGnzrBz6ithQIeOIxaY=s4800-w800
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* add source - https://www.blackwatercastle.com/

*Description:*
Blackwater Castle, also and formerly known as Castle Widenham, is a privately owned estate located in the village of Castletownroche between the towns of Mallow and Fermoy in north County Cork, Ireland. Since 2005 it has been available to rent as a private hire venue for castle weddings and private parties along with castle rentals for vacations.

_Listing 3 of 19_

*Region:* the heartlands

*Period:* 13th century

*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Roscrea_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Roscrea%20Castle/@52.9534,-7.801,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZNmmJqAWhI3yOf8FSCwvQMDbJ8crAsO-PvgG1TRnhmYXEcLo95oCoLvKEFbcnVf0cExfwNPTh7WhYraSsBOpgkq1ryfLo0Hrco5Lf0tSU2f_lyRJt2Br69K7PPaZRC00svpR9950fuNOGIAXg=s4800-w800

*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* update google places - https://maps.app.goo.gl/jtaoWW85jkfNjs5s8


*Description:*
Roscrea Castle is a 13th-century motte-and-bailey castle in the town of Roscrea, Ireland. The castle consists of a walled courtyard, gate block, and angled towers. Along with 18th century Damer House and gardens, the castle forms part of Roscrea Heritage Centre.

_Listing 4 of 19_

*Region:* the heartlands
*County:* County Tipperary
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 16th century
*Coords:* 57.55361111, -4.18
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ormond_Castle
*Maps:* https://maps.app.goo.gl/keAmmjFPvuZqvdHw6
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZP2f3cySbiV2xlO3jUAnzeXDBn1z_XnoIe9dPYPiYbKMMUH6LrjPTLFQz_I2m43vYRw0snBkjoDHg6i1ikfbxBfecx4Iv8cj94g2DYkJaN_iBoM-V-JTycKTD2339--mt38NSWZ26hZSRKJaBU=s4800-w800
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* more reseaerch needed 

*Description:*
Ormond Castle, also known as Avoch Castle, was a powerful stronghold, overlooking the village of Avoch, on the Black Isle, in the former county of Ross and Cromarty, now part of Highland, Scotland. It controlled a prominent position overlooking the Moray Firth and critical to the sea access to Inverness.

_Listing 5 of 19_
